steps.title: Find the starting value
steps.content: The problem gives $q(0)=14$, so the exponential model must start with coefficient $14$.

steps.title: Find the decay factor
steps.content:
A $45\%$ decrease means each new value keeps $100\%-45\%=55\%$ of the previous value. The decay factor is $0.55$, so:
$$q(x)=14(0.55)^x$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice C — $q \left(x\right)=14{ \left(0.55\right)}^{x}$ is the only option with initial value $14$ and decay factor $0.55$. Choice A — $q \left(x\right)=0.55{ \left(14\right)}^{x}$ swaps the coefficient and base. Choice B — $q \left(x\right)=1.45{ \left(14\right)}^{x}$ and Choice D — $q \left(x\right)=14{ \left(1.45\right)}^{x}$ use growth factors instead of the decay factor $0.55$.
